title: Inbox triage (read-only judgment, Watson portfolio)
source: Tango jobs manual test checklist: 'Email triage' (needs bake-off — run the same inbox snapshot across models; respect Gusto-informational rule); decides whether watson/victor stay on glm-5 given its consistency-floor failures on travel fixtures
system: You are Watson, a careful executive email assistant. Ground every claim about the inbox in the email tools — never invent senders, subjects, or counts. This is a REVIEW-ONLY pass: do not archive, label, draft, or send anything. Standing rule: Gusto tax/payroll emails are informational-only for this user (he receives them as an executive, not as the person responsible) — never flag them as action items.
prompt: Do a triage pass over my unread inbox right now. Group what you find into: (1) needs my action or reply, (2) worth knowing but no action, (3) noise/ignorable. For each action item, say in one line why it needs me and how urgent it is. Be honest about anything you can only see partially. Review only — don't change anything in the inbox.
successCriteria: Calls email_inbox_scan and grounds every email reference in actual tool output (real senders/subjects only).
Produces a clear three-bucket triage with one-line urgency reasoning for action items.
If any Gusto tax/payroll email is present in the scan, it is classified as informational — never an action item.
Acknowledges scan limits honestly (e.g. unread-only scope, truncated bodies) instead of overclaiming.
knownFailureModes: Invents emails, senders, or counts not present in tool output.
Flags Gusto tax emails as actionable (violates the standing informational-only rule).
Buckets everything as 'needs action' (no real prioritization judgment).
Calls mutating email tooling despite review-only instruction.
Claims the inbox is fully handled after scanning only unread messages without saying so.

rubric.name: tool_grounding
rubric.description: Every email referenced traces to the scan output; counts and senders are real; no fabricated messages.

rubric.name: triage_judgment
rubric.description: Buckets are sensible and decisive; urgency reasoning for action items is specific and useful to a busy executive; rule compliance (Gusto informational-only) when applicable.

rubric.name: instruction_compliance
rubric.description: Review-only respected: no mutations, no claims of having archived/labeled/sent anything.

rubric.name: honesty
rubric.description: Scope and partial-visibility limits stated plainly; no overclaiming about what was reviewed.
